scope: Google Search Console technical SEO signals and prior connected-user ARIA regression

dataState.local: existing local application database plus deterministic test fixtures

findings.before: Production redirected the apex host to www, while the home page, terms page and privacy page declared apex-host canonicals. The live sitemap already used www.
findings.cause: Static HTML and the local default public origin still used https://topykly.com even though the production platform selected https://www.topykly.com as the permanent destination.
findings.fix: Aligned home, legal, Open Graph, Twitter, WebSite JSON-LD, topic-sharing fallback and default server origin with https://www.topykly.com.
evidence: Production before fix: https://topykly.com/ returned 301 to https://www.topykly.com/.
Production before fix: https://www.topykly.com/ declared canonical https://topykly.com/.
Production before fix: the www terms page declared canonical https://topykly.com/terms.html.
Local after fix: home, /temas, /archivo, /terms.html and /privacy.html exactly match their sitemap URLs.

findings.before: robots.txt blocked /dashboard.html even though the URL existed as an internal server path, blocked public avatar images referenced by profile metadata, and the sitemap omitted the indexable privacy page.
findings.cause: The crawler policy and sitemap evolved separately from the public page and structured-data surface.
findings.fix: Kept /api/ blocked, removed the dashboard and avatar crawler blocks, added privacy.html to the sitemap, and covered all expected directives with deterministic tests.
evidence: Local robots.txt now contains only Disallow: /api/ plus the canonical www sitemap URL.
Local sitemap includes home, topics, archive, terms and privacy URLs on the www host.
The sitemap test still excludes thin topics and profiles that opted out of indexing.

findings.before: Production served mascot-review.html, the files 1000 and taskkill, and internal verification screenshots with successful 200 responses.
findings.cause: The static server protected private directories and extensions but did not distinguish several root-level development artifacts from public assets.
findings.fix: Added an explicit unpublished root-file set so development HTML, scratch files and verification screenshots return 404.
evidence: Production before fix: /1000 and /taskkill returned 200 application/octet-stream.
Local after fix: dashboard.html, mascot-review.html, 1000, taskkill and both root verification screenshots return 404.
Server regression tests cover every unpublished path.

findings.before: The application shell had no h1 in its source document.
findings.cause: The visual brand was exposed as an image role and application sections started at h2/h3.
findings.fix: Added one visually hidden, descriptive h1 without changing the visual layout.
evidence: Chromium desktop and mobile accessibility snapshots expose one level-1 heading: TOPYKLY: comunidad de temas y rankings en español.
The existing sr-only utility keeps the heading available to crawlers and assistive technology without visual movement.

findings.before: The desktop connected-user list and mobile drawer rendered the same user with an identical user-action-menu-{user.id} ID.
findings.cause: createUserItem derived the menu ID only from the user ID, while renderUsers intentionally renders each user into both targets.
findings.fix: Added the target container ID as a menu namespace and kept each trigger's aria-controls synchronized with its corresponding menu.
evidence: The UI regression test verifies distinct IDs and matching aria-controls relationships.
The full UI suite passes with the prior local fix preserved.

performance.reason: The implemented changes affect metadata, sitemap generation, crawler policy, static route eligibility and one visually hidden heading; they do not materially alter runtime rendering or network weight.

verification.fullQa: passed: 172 deterministic tests and 12 smoke tests
verification.productionAudit: completed before deployment; canonical conflict and public artifacts remain live until release

residualRisks: The fixes must be deployed before Search Console can observe them.
Search Console may retain old exclusion or canonical statuses until Google recrawls the affected URLs and sitemap.

The exact Search Console issue export was not available in the workspace, so this audit targeted reproducible technical defects from the live site rather than matching private console row labels one by one.
